sourceMovie.title: Inherent Vice
sourceMovie.whyPeopleLoveIt: Inherent Vice (2014) stuck because it commits to its drama premise without apologizing—viewers remember set pieces and momentum as much as plot. Paul Thomas Anderson's control of tone keeps the ride coherent. The drama and mystery blend is the engine: it lets the film shift registers without feeling like two different movies stitched together.

recommendations.title: Fear and Loathing in Las Vegas
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: If Inherent Vice clicked for you, Fear and Loathing in Las Vegas (1998) is a strong next stop: both trade in drama and comedy, with comic timing and sharper banter. Expect a different story, but a similar willingness to go big when the moment demands it.

recommendations.title: A Walk Among the Tombstones
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: If Inherent Vice clicked for you, A Walk Among the Tombstones (2014) is a strong next stop: both trade in drama and mystery, with unease that lingers after the credits. Expect a different story, but a similar willingness to go big when the moment demands it.

faq.question: What should I watch after Inherent Vice?
faq.answer: Try Fear and Loathing in Las Vegas, A Walk Among the Tombstones, Brick—each shares DNA with Inherent Vice while changing the setting enough to feel fresh.

faq.question: What genre is Inherent Vice?
faq.answer: Inherent Vice (2014) is best described as Drama, Mystery, Comedy. Stores and algorithms use different labels, but that mix is what fans actually recognize on a rewatch.

faq.question: Is Inherent Vice worth revisiting?
faq.answer: Viewer scores sit around 6.5/10 on TMDB—high enough that replays are common for people who like this drama lane.
